careerPath.title: AI Infrastructure Engineer
careerPath.description: Learn to design, deploy, and operate the infrastructure that powers AI systems in production.

stages.title: Foundations
stages.description: Core Linux, networking, and programming skills every infra engineer needs.

milestones.title: Linux & Command Line Essentials
milestones.description: Navigate, script, and manage processes on Linux systems.

skills.title: Shell scripting
skills.description: Write bash scripts using variables, loops, conditionals, and functions to automate repetitive server tasks.

skills.title: File system & permissions
skills.description: Understand Linux file ownership, permission bits (rwx), and commands like chmod/chown to control access safely.

skills.title: Process management
skills.description: Inspect, background, and kill processes; understand PIDs, signals, and how to keep long-running services alive.

questions.prompt: Which command changes file permissions?
Which command lists running processes?
Which command changes file ownership?
What does the command 'kill -9 <pid>' do?
Which of these are valid ways to run a script in the background?
Which permission value grants read, write, and execute to the owner only?
What shell command shows the current working directory? (3 letters)
What does a shebang line like #!/bin/bash do?
options: Specifies which interpreter runs the script
questions.correctAnswer: Specifies which interpreter runs the script

projects.title: Server Setup Script
projects.description: Write a bash script that provisions a fresh Ubuntu server with common dev tooling.
rubric: Runs without errors on a clean VM

milestones.title: Networking Basics
milestones.description: Understand DNS, HTTP, TCP/IP, and how services talk to each other.

skills.title: DNS resolution
skills.description: Understand how domain names resolve to IP addresses, including A/CNAME records and how DNS lookups are cached.

skills.title: HTTP/HTTPS
skills.description: Understand request/response cycles, status codes, headers, and how TLS secures HTTP traffic.

skills.title: TCP/IP basics
skills.description: Understand how data moves across networks: IP addressing, ports, and the handshake behind reliable connections.

skills.title: Load balancing concepts
skills.description: Understand how traffic gets distributed across multiple servers, and common strategies like round-robin and health checks.

questions.prompt: What port does HTTPS use by default?
What port does HTTP use by default?
Which DNS record type maps a domain name to an IPv4 address?
Which of these are valid HTTP methods?
What does TCP guarantee that UDP does not?
What HTTP status code indicates a successful request?
What protocol secures HTTP traffic with encryption? (abbreviation)
What is the primary purpose of a load balancer?

stages.title: Cloud & MLOps
stages.description: Deploy and manage AI workloads on cloud infrastructure.

milestones.title: Containers & Docker
milestones.description: Package and run applications with Docker.

skills.title: Dockerfile authoring
skills.description: Write Dockerfiles that build reproducible images: base images, layers, COPY/RUN instructions, and entrypoints.

skills.title: Image optimization
skills.description: Reduce image size and build time using multi-stage builds, layer caching, and minimal base images.

skills.title: Container networking
skills.description: Understand how containers communicate with each other and the host via bridge networks, ports, and DNS.

questions.prompt: Which file defines how a Docker image is built?
Which instruction in a Dockerfile copies files into the image?
What technique reduces final image size by separating build and runtime stages?
Which of these help reduce Docker image size?
options: Removing package manager caches after install
correctAnswer: Removing package manager caches after install

questions.prompt: What command builds an image from a Dockerfile in the current directory?
What command lists all running containers? (2 words)
What is the purpose of a .dockerignore file?
options: Exclude files from the build context
questions.correctAnswer: Exclude files from the build context

projects.title: Containerize a Model Serving API
projects.description: Package a simple ML inference API into a production-ready Docker image.

milestones.title: Orchestration with Kubernetes
milestones.description: Deploy and scale containerized workloads with Kubernetes.

skills.title: Pods & Deployments
skills.description: Understand Kubernetes' smallest deployable unit (Pod) and how Deployments manage replicas and rollouts.

skills.title: Services & Ingress
skills.description: Expose Pods internally (Services) and externally (Ingress) with stable networking and routing rules.

skills.title: Autoscaling
skills.description: Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscalers to automatically adjust replica count based on load.

skills.title: ConfigMaps & Secrets
skills.description: Externalize configuration and sensitive values from container images using Kubernetes-native objects.

questions.prompt: What Kubernetes object exposes a set of Pods as a network service?
What is the smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes?
Which object manages rolling updates and replica counts for Pods?
Which of these are used to store configuration or sensitive data in Kubernetes?
What does a Horizontal Pod Autoscaler adjust?
What Kubernetes object manages external HTTP routing to Services?
What CLI tool is used to interact with a Kubernetes cluster? (abbreviation)

milestones.title: CI/CD for ML Systems
milestones.description: Automate testing, building, and deployment of AI infrastructure.

skills.title: GitHub Actions
skills.description: Write workflow YAML to automate builds, tests, and deployments triggered by pushes or pull requests.

skills.title: Automated testing
skills.description: Integrate test suites into a pipeline so code changes are validated automatically before merge.

skills.title: Deployment pipelines
skills.description: Chain build, test, and deploy stages together so a merge to main safely ships to production.

questions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a CI pipeline?
options: Automatically test and validate code changes
questions.correctAnswer: Automatically test and validate code changes

questions.prompt: What does CD typically stand for in CI/CD?
Which of these are common stages in a CI/CD pipeline?
What is a rollback strategy used for?
options: Reverting to a previous working version after a bad deploy
questions.correctAnswer: Reverting to a previous working version after a bad deploy

questions.prompt: In GitHub Actions, what file defines a workflow?
What triggers a typical CI run on GitHub Actions?
What term describes automatically deploying every change that passes tests, with no manual approval step?

projects.title: End-to-End Deployment Pipeline
projects.description: Build a CI/CD pipeline that tests, builds, and deploys a containerized service on push to main.

stages.title: Scaling & Observability
stages.description: Monitor, scale, and harden AI infrastructure for production reliability.

milestones.title: Monitoring & Observability
milestones.description: Set up metrics, logging, and alerting for production systems.

skills.title: Metrics collection
skills.description: Instrument services to emit metrics (latency, error rate, throughput) and scrape them with tools like Prometheus.

skills.title: Structured logging
skills.description: Emit logs as structured JSON with consistent fields so they're searchable and machine-parseable at scale.

skills.title: Alerting & on-call basics
skills.description: Set thresholds that trigger alerts, and understand on-call fundamentals like escalation and runbooks.

questions.prompt: Which of these is a common open-source metrics & alerting tool?
What is structured logging?
options: Logs written in plain free-form text
Logs emitted in a consistent, machine-parseable format like JSON
questions.correctAnswer: Logs emitted in a consistent, machine-parseable format like JSON

questions.prompt: Which of these are considered 'golden signals' for monitoring a service?
What is the purpose of an alerting threshold?
options: To trigger a notification when a metric crosses a defined limit
questions.correctAnswer: To trigger a notification when a metric crosses a defined limit

questions.prompt: What is a runbook used for during an incident?
options: A documented set of steps to diagnose and resolve a known issue
questions.correctAnswer: A documented set of steps to diagnose and resolve a known issue

questions.prompt: What popular visualization tool is commonly paired with Prometheus for dashboards?

milestones.title: Cost Management & Scaling
milestones.description: Balance performance, reliability, and cost when scaling AI infrastructure.

skills.title: Autoscaling strategies
skills.description: Compare horizontal vs vertical scaling and choose strategies based on cost and traffic patterns.

skills.title: Cost monitoring
skills.description: Track cloud spend against usage to catch runaway costs before they become a problem.

skills.title: Capacity planning
skills.description: Forecast future resource needs based on growth trends to avoid both over- and under-provisioning.

questions.prompt: What is horizontal scaling?
What is vertical scaling?
options: Increasing the resources (CPU/RAM) of an existing machine
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the resources (CPU/RAM) of an existing machine

questions.prompt: Which of these help control cloud costs?
What is capacity planning primarily concerned with?
options: Forecasting future resource needs based on growth trends
questions.correctAnswer: Forecasting future resource needs based on growth trends

questions.prompt: Which pricing model typically costs less in exchange for committing to usage over time?
What term describes automatically scaling resources down to zero when idle?

projects.title: Capstone: Production-Ready AI Service
projects.description: Combine containers, orchestration, CI/CD, and monitoring into one deployed, observable AI inference service.
